details: Atlasora-config@1.0.0 is a trojanized package that steals credentials at install time via a postinstall hook. The file install.js is executed automatically after npm install. It harvests environment variables (including COINBASE_API_KEY, COINBASE_API_SECRET, OPENAI_API_KEY, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, REVOLUT_API_SECRET_KEY, PRIVATE_KEY, MNEMONIC, DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, and others). It reads.env files from the current and parent directories, SSH private keys from ~/.ssh/, AWS credentials from ~/.aws/credentials, NPM tokens from ~/.npmrc, and git configuration. All harvested data is exfiltrated via HTTPS POST to webhook[.]site/22e20640-e2a1-4bb2-b203-061077d055ff. Errors are silently caught so the npm install appears to succeed without alerting the user. The package name is a combosquat of the legitimate AtlasOra platform names.
